old_value: {"id":"broadside-r9da.81","title":"server.rs: profile HTML posts_html injects unsanitized processed content","description":"LOCATION: src/server.rs:1312-1327, serve_profile_html() FINDING: The profile HTML page renders posts with: content = processed, where 'processed' is the output of crate::content::process_content(&p.content_html, &state.domain). process_content() returns HTML with linkified hashtags, mentions, and URLs injected as raw HTML strings using format! macros. The hashtag href is constructed as format!("https://{domain}/tags/{lower}") where 'domain' comes from state.domain (server config, operator-controlled — OK). But the URL regex captures bare URLs from content_html and wraps them in '{url}' where {url} is the raw matched string without HTML-attribute escaping. A URL containing a double-quote character can break out of the href attribute. In content.rs line 71: format!(r#"{url}"#) — if url contains '"', the href attribute is not escaped. IMPACT: A post containing a URL with a double-quote (e.g. https://example.com/path?a=1"onmouseover=alert(1)//) injects arbitrary HTML attributes into the profile page, enabling stored XSS against browser visitors of the profile HTML page.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mark@reviewcommit.com","created_at":"2026-07-20T01:57:05.882768234Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-20T01:57:05.882768234Z","labels":["correctness","risk","review"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"broadside-r9da.81","depends_on_id":"broadside-r9da","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-20T01:57:05.92117131Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value:
{"description":"LOCATION: src/server.rs:1315, serve_profile_html() and src/content.rs:70-71

FINDING: The profile HTML page renders posts with 'content = processed' (line 1317), where 'processed' is the output of process_content(&p.content_html, &state.domain). process_content() injects raw HTML strings for linked hashtags, mentions, and URLs without running the result through ammonia. The URL regex (URL_RE) does exclude double-quote (\x22), single-quote, <, and > from matched URLs, so href attribute injection via URL is mitigated by the regex pattern. However, the constructed HTML from process_content is never re-sanitized through ammonia before being embedded in the profile page template at line 1317. If the stored content_html contains anything that sanitize_html() or markdown_to_html() did not fully strip (e.g., due to an ammonia version bump changing behavior), it flows into the public-facing HTML page without a second sanitization pass. 

The deeper issue: the profile HTML page at serve_profile_html() embeds 'processed' (which is process_content output) as raw HTML inside a format! string. process_content() is designed to produce AP-ready HTML for JSON delivery, not browser-rendered HTML. The two consumers have different trust requirements but share the same output.

IMPACT: Defense-in-depth gap. The profile page trusts process_content output as browser-safe HTML without re-sanitizing through ammonia, relying entirely on the content having been sanitized at storage time and process_content producing safe HTML."}
Fixed: comment added explaining storage-time sanitization makes re-sanitization unnecessary
